items.headline: Mountain View updates biodiversity and urban forest plan
items.summary: The City Council reviewed a draft plan to improve biodiversity and urban forest management in Mountain View. This plan could affect how the city manages parks, trees, and green spaces that residents use and enjoy.

items.headline: Mixed-use development proposed: 67 homes and retail in Sunnyvale
items.summary: A 4.22-acre site would be developed with 55 townhomes, 12 apartments, and retail space including a new two-story building, while preserving the existing Speedy's Tacos location. The project is under study session review, meaning the Planning Commission is examining it before making a decision.

items.headline: Campbell updates business license tax rates
items.summary: The City Council considered revisions to business license tax rates under Campbell Municipal Code. The outcome and specific rate changes were not detailed in the available agenda information.

items.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026: City considers temporary supergraphic signs near Levi's Stadium
items.summary: Santa Clara was set to vote on allowing large supergraphic signs on building surfaces around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup events, though the item was withdrawn by staff. This would have temporarily changed the visual appearance of the stadium area during the tournament.

items.headline: Milpitas adopts FY 2026-27 development fees and user charges
items.summary: City Council approved updated development impact fees and user charges for the fiscal year ahead. These fees affect costs for new construction projects and city services, potentially impacting housing and commercial development affordability in Milpitas.

items.headline: Santa Clara County homelessness plan presented to Milpitas council
items.summary: County officials presented their Community Plan to End Homelessness to the City Council on April 21st. This regional initiative could shape local homelessness response efforts and resource allocation in Milpitas.

items.headline: Gas utility rates being adjusted for fiscal year 2027
items.summary: The Council is adopting updated rate schedules for residential and commercial gas service. Residents should expect potential changes to their natural gas bills starting in the new fiscal year.

items.headline: City Council reviews building permit process and timelines
items.summary: The council discussed a guide on San José's development process, from initial planning through occupancy, including permitting requirements and coordination procedures. This could be useful for residents or businesses planning construction projects in the city.
